sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: ALTRIA GROUP, INC.는 2025-06-30 분기에 매출 61억 200만 달러를 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 연초 대비 조정 순이익은 45억 2,200만 달러 수준으로, 일회성 비용을 제외한 기초 체력은 안정적인 흐름을 보임
최고운영책임자(CODM)는 일반 기업 비용과 무형자산 상각을 제외한 OCI를 통해 세그먼트별 성과를 평가하고 자원을 배분함

riskChanges.text: 불법 전자담배 및 니코틴 파우치 제품의 시장 범람과 FDA의 규제 대응 미흡이 담배 제품의 출하량 감소 및 시장 경쟁 심화의 주요 원인으로 작용함
거시경제적 요인(인플레이션, 관세)과 소비자의 구매 행동 변화로 인해 저가 담배 제품으로의 전환이 가속화되며 출하량에 하방 압력을 가하고 있음

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 6월 30일 기준 매출 61억 200만 달러, 영업이익 32억 3,000만 달러, 순이익 23억 7,800만 달러를 달성함
총자산 323억 3,200만 달러 규모를 보유하고 있으며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 12억 8,700만 달러 수준임

watchItems.text: 규제 환경 변화와 불법 제품 유입에 따른 출하량 감소 리스크가 상존하므로, 가격 결정력을 통한 마진 방어 능력이 향후 실적의 핵심 관전 포인트임

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-06-30 revenue = 61억 200만 달러 (USD 6.102B; 6,102 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 operating income = 32억 3,000만 달러 (USD 3.23B; 3,230 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 net income = 23억 7,800만 달러 (USD 2.378B; 2,378 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 total assets = 323억 3,200만 달러 (USD 32.332B; 32,332 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 cash and cash equivalents = 12억 8,700만 달러 (USD 1.287B; 1,287 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.

Item 1A. Risk Factors in our 2024 Form 10-K and in Part II, Item 1A. Risk Factors of our Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (“First Quarter Form 10-Q”), include: ▪ pending and threatened litigation and bonding requirements; ▪ restrictions and requirements imposed by the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act (“FSPTCA”) and restrictions and requirements (and related enforcement actions) that have been, and in the future will be, imposed by the FDA; ▪ the FDA’s failure to effectively address illicit tobacco products on the market, including illicit e-vapor and oral nicotine pouch products; ▪ illicit trade in tobacco products, including cigarettes, e-vapor products and oral nicotine pouch products; ▪ actual and proposed excise tax increases, as well as changes in tax structures and tax stamping requirements; ▪ bans and restrictions on tobacco use i

consumers through third-party digital platforms; ▪ changes in adult tobacco consumer purchase behavior, which is influenced by various factors such as macroeconomic conditions (including inflation and tariffs), the proliferation of illicit disposable e-vapor products, excise taxes and product price gap relationships, each of which has resulted and may in the future result in adult tobacco consumers switching to lower-priced tobacco products and lower shipment volumes; ▪ the highly competitive nature of all tobacco categories, including competitive disadvantages related to the impact on cigarette prices due to the settlement of certain healthcare cost recovery litigation and the growth of innovative tobacco products, such as e-vapor and oral nicotine pouch products; ▪ the growth of products using nicotine analogues that are designed to imitate the effects of nicotine but are not subject
